title: Renewal date 30 days out (Snowflake DATEADD)
prompt: You're writing Snowflake SQL. Each subscription renews 30 days after it started. Using Snowflake's DATEADD, return the customer and the renewal date. Columns: customer, renews_on. Order by renews_on, then customer.

generic: SELECT customer, started_at + INTERVAL 30 DAY AS renews_on
snowflake: SELECT customer, DATEADD('day', 30, started_at) AS renews_on

requires.message: Use Snowflake's DATEADD, not interval arithmetic.

hints: DATEADD('day', 30, started_at) shifts the start date forward by 30 days.
Order by the computed renews_on, breaking ties by customer.
